downstream_consumers: Every CODEREF-CORE agent session (auto-loads CONTEXT.md — the highest-read surface)
Any operator/agent following rag-index/rag-search/rag-status/coderef-scan skill docs (currently mis-documented CLI interfaces fail)

risks.title: Doc edits drift again as code evolves
risks.mitigation: Prefer regenerating foundation-docs from the generator over hand-editing; cite the close commit (4cd33af) as the reality anchor so future audits have a fixed reference.

risks.title: RAG reindex is long-running (~9.7h full)
risks.mitigation: Use incremental reindex (exercises P0 incremental-truth fix); run in a dedicated phase with a hard stop before + after so the operator controls when the multi-hour job runs. Ollama-local only.

risks.title: Editing ASSISTANT canonical skill files can trip semantic-validator substring false-positive (openai/anthropic) — ISS-002 5th recurrence in the parent WO
risks.mitigation: Override-with-evidence per established precedent (STUB-6MM7W2); do not re-mint.

risks.title: Cross-repo commit confusion (two repos, main-only, explicit-path)
risks.mitigation: Commit each repo separately by explicit path; never git add -A; CODEREF-CORE gets docs + .mcp.json + WO artifacts, ASSISTANT gets skills + CONTEXT.md.

risks.title: Two open stubs carry now-unsatisfiable analyzer-service.ts constraints
risks.mitigation: Reconcile (supersede or annotate) rather than attempt to satisfy a constraint against deleted code.

dependencies: coderef/workorder/repo-review-2026-07-remediation/post-close-verification-2026-07-02.txt (the reality anchor — enumerates every stale claim)
rag-index / rag-status CLI bins (for the reindex phase; ollama-local)
coderef/foundation-docs generator (for foundation-docs regeneration, if present)
git history (for .mcp.json restore via git checkout --)

decision_records.decision: Fold RAG reindex + .mcp.json restore INTO this WO (operator chose scope=everything)
decision_records.rationale: Operator explicitly selected 'Everything' — one coherent reality-sync unit rather than three separate follow-ups.

decision_records.decision: Plan-type strict (operator choice, matches fix-category default)
decision_records.rationale: Per-phase hard stops let the operator review each surface (CORE docs, ASSISTANT skills, CONTEXT.md, reindex, .mcp.json) before the next; the multi-hour reindex especially wants a gate.
Foundation-docs are generator-produced; hand-editing re-introduces drift. Regeneration from current code is the durable fix.
